title: [lora_l2.c:484] lichen_lora_l2_init idempotency check outside mutex creates observation window
description:
Contract: lichen_lora_l2_init is documented as idempotent.
Violation: The function acquires mutex (line 498) then checks state (line 502). A caller that doesn't hold the mutex could observe intermediate states. For example, thread A is inside init() generating EUI-64, thread B calls init(), acquires mutex, sees state != UNINIT (because A already transitioned), returns success - but A hasn't finished generating EUI-64 yet. Wait, no - A holds the mutex, so B blocks until A releases. Let me re-read...
Actually: The current implementation IS correct. Thread A holds mutex through the entire init. Thread B blocks at mutex acquisition. When A releases, B sees final state and returns correctly. The comment at lines 488-497 explains this correctly.
Impact: None - this is not actually a bug. The implementation is correct.
Mitigation: No action needed - closing as false positive.
